name: pysam (BAM/SAM/VCF File Processing for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Andreas Heger and contributors

auth.notes: Local library; htslib backend; no auth for local files

performance.notes: htslib C backend; random access via BAI index; whole-genome iteration in minutes

agent_readiness.notes: Python wrapper for htslib — reads/writes BAM, SAM, CRAM, VCF, BCF. import pysam. bam = pysam.AlignmentFile('reads.bam', 'rb'). for read in bam.fetch('chr1', 1000, 5000): read.query_name; read.query_sequence; read.get_tag('NM'). vcf = pysam.VariantFile('variants.vcf'). for rec in vcf.fetch('chr1'): rec.chrom; rec.pos; rec.ref; rec.alts. pysam.sort(); pysam.index(). Coverage: bam.count_coverage('chr1', 0, 100). Pileup for variant calling. Used in GATK, DeepVariant pipelines. Random access via tabix index.

gotchas: BAM files must be sorted and indexed (.bai) for random access fetch()
VCF positions are 1-based in format but 0-based in pysam Python API
Memory management — AlignedSegment objects should not be held beyond loop iteration
CRAM requires reference genome available locally
best_when: BAM/SAM read processing in Python genomics pipelines
Coverage depth calculation at genomic intervals
Building custom variant callers or annotation tools
avoid_when: Simple BAM statistics — samtools flagstat/idxstats is faster
Large-scale population genomics (Hail or Spark-based tools scale better)
summary: pysam wraps htslib for Python genomics. AlignmentFile('reads.bam', 'rb').fetch('chr1', 1000, 5000) iterates reads in BAM/SAM/CRAM. VariantFile('variants.vcf').fetch() iterates VCF/BCF records. Coverage: bam.count_coverage(). pysam.sort(), pysam.index(). C backend via htslib. BAM must be indexed for random access. Used in GATK, DeepVariant. Essential for any Python genomics pipeline that processes aligned reads.
